message.content:
<teammate-message teammate_id="team-lead" summary="Worker-5: T5 State Machine impl">
You are worker-5, member of team dev-taskflow-m1m2m3m6.
Superior: Team Lead (TL).
Allowed contacts: TL only.
Do NOT SendMessage anyone else. No peer communication.

YOUR TASK:
T5: M3 - Task State Machine

Implement state transition logic and add status change + transitions query endpoints to TaskController.

Acceptance criteria (M3-AC1-16):
- TaskStateMachine: valid transitions: TODO→[IN_PROGRESS], IN_PROGRESS→[TODO,DONE], DONE→[CLOSED], CLOSED→[] (terminal)
- Methods: canTransition(from, to) → boolean, getAvailableTransitions(status) → List<TaskStatus>
- PATCH /api/tasks/{id}/status: validate transition, update status + updatedAt, return TaskResponse (200). Error 400 "Cannot transition from X to Y"
- GET /api/tasks/{id}/transitions: return TransitionResponse {currentStatus, availableTransitions} (200)
- StatusChangeRequest: status (enum TaskStatus, required)
- TransitionResponse: currentStatus (String), availableTransitions (List<String>)
- 401 without token, 404 for nonexistent task

File Scope:
- ALLOWED: backend/src/main/java/com/taskflow/service/TaskStateMachine.java, backend/src/main/java/com/taskflow/dto/StatusChangeRequest.java, backend/src/main/java/com/taskflow/dto/TransitionResponse.java, backend/src/main/java/com/taskflow/controller/TaskController.java (modify — add 2 new endpoints)
- READONLY: backend/src/main/java/com/taskflow/entity/Task.java, backend/src/main/java/com/taskflow/repository/TaskRepository.java, backend/src/main/java/com/taskflow/dto/TaskResponse.java, backend/src/main/java/com/taskflow/service/TaskService.java, backend/src/main/java/com/taskflow/controller/AuthController.java, backend/src/main/java/com/taskflow/service/AuthService.java
- Everything else: FORBIDDEN

REQUIRED READING (do this FIRST):
1. PLAN: C:\Users\a0304\IdeaProjects\TouchFish-DevTeam\taskflow\docs\dev-team\2026-03-06-180000\2026-03-06-PLAN.md
2. CONTRACT: C:\Users\a0304\IdeaProjects\TouchFish-DevTeam\taskflow\docs\dev-team\2026-03-06-180000\2026-03-06-CONTRACT.md
3. Test files: backend/src/test/java/com/taskflow/service/TaskStateMachineTest.java, backend/src/test/java/com/taskflow/controller/StatusTransitionIntegrationTest.java
4. Implementation notes: C:\Users\a0304\IdeaProjects\TouchFish-DevTeam\taskflow\docs\dev-team\2026-03-06-180000\temp\impl-notes-5.md
5. ALL READONLY files (especially TaskController.java to understand existing structure, Task.java for enums, TaskService.java for patterns)

KEY DETAILS:
- TaskStateMachine must have no-arg constructor (unit tests instantiate with `new TaskStateMachine()`)
- Make it a @Component so it can be injected into TaskController
- Error message: "Cannot transition from {FROM} to {TO}"
- Use TaskRepository.findByIdAndDeletedFalse(id) for task lookup
- Set task.setUpdatedAt(LocalDateTime.now()) on successful transition
- TransitionResponse.availableTransitions uses status.name() for String conversion
- PATCH returns TaskResponse (reuse existing DTO conversion pattern from TaskService)
- Add endpoints to EXISTING TaskController — do NOT create a new controller

PARALLEL AWARENESS:
No other workers running. You are the last implementation task.
Run YOUR test files:
- cd C:/Users/a0304/IdeaProjects/TouchFish-DevTeam/taskflow/backend
- ./mvnw test -Dtest="TaskStateMachineTest,StatusTransitionIntegrationTest" -pl . 2>&1 | tail -80

LOG WRITING:
Write to C:\Users\a0304\IdeaProjects\TouchFish-DevTeam\taskflow\docs\dev-team\2026-03-06-180000\logs\worker-5.log.md (append-only)

COMPLETION SEQUENCE:
1. Verify all 37 tests pass
1.5. Run `git diff --name-only` → compare against ALLOWED list. Revert out-of-scope.
2. Write final log entries
3. SendMessage TL: task ID, status, tests passed/failed, changed files list
4. Wait for TL response, then approve shutdown when requested
</teammate-message>

1→[SECTION] Project Overview
     2→[GOAL] Implement M1 (JWT Auth) + M2 (Task CRUD) + M3 (Task State Machine) + M6 (Task List Page) for TaskFlow
     3→[ARCH] Spring Boot 3.2.3 + H2 in-memory | Vue 3 + Pinia + axios | JJWT 0.12.5 | Java 17 | Vite 5 + Vitest
     4→[NAMING] Java: camelCase fields, PascalCase classes, kebab-case endpoints | Vue: PascalCase components, camelCase props/state | Package: com.taskflow.{entity,repository,service,controller,dto,security,config}
     5→[CONSTRAINTS] ≤5 ALLOWED files per task | Soft delete (no hard delete) | JWT 24h expiry | H2 in-memory (create-drop) | No Lombok (not in pom.xml)
     6→[SHARED] backend/src/main/java/com/taskflow/entity/User.java | backend/src/main/java/com/taskflow/entity/Task.java | backend/src/main/java/com/taskflow/controller/TaskController.java | frontend/src/stores/taskStore.ts
     7→
     8→[SECTION] Cross-Cutting Concerns
     9→[CROSS-CUTTING] error-format=JSON {message:string} + HTTP status | jwt-filter=OncePerRequestFilter before UsernamePasswordAuthenticationFilter | soft-delete=boolean deleted field, repo queries filter deleted=false | validation=Jakarta @Valid annotations, MethodArgumentNotValidException → 400 | axios-interceptor=auto-attach Bearer token from localStorage

[SECTION] Project Overview
[GOAL] Implement M1 (JWT Auth) + M2 (Task CRUD) + M3 (Task State Machine) + M6 (Task List Page) for TaskFlow
[ARCH] Spring Boot 3.2.3 + H2 in-memory | Vue 3 + Pinia + axios | JJWT 0.12.5 | Java 17 | Vite 5 + Vitest
[NAMING] Java: camelCase fields, PascalCase classes, kebab-case endpoints | Vue: PascalCase components, camelCase props/state | Package: com.taskflow.{entity,repository,service,controller,dto,security,config}
[CONSTRAINTS] ≤5 ALLOWED files per task | Soft delete (no hard delete) | JWT 24h expiry | H2 in-memory (create-drop) | No Lombok (not in pom.xml)
[SHARED] backend/src/main/java/com/taskflow/entity/User.java | backend/src/main/java/com/taskflow/entity/Task.java | backend/src/main/java/com/taskflow/controller/TaskController.java | frontend/src/stores/taskStore.ts

[SECTION] Cross-Cutting Concerns
[CROSS-CUTTING] error-format=JSON {message:string} + HTTP status | jwt-filter=OncePerRequestFilter before UsernamePasswordAuthenticationFilter | soft-delete=boolean deleted field, repo queries filter deleted=false | validation=Jakarta @Valid annotations, MethodArgumentNotValidException → 400 | axios-interceptor=auto-attach Bearer token from localStorage

[SECTION] Standards
[SOURCE] none
[OVERRIDE] follow-actual-code | SecurityConfig exists with permitAll /api/auth/**, STATELESS sessions, CSRF disabled

[SECTION] Specs
[SPEC] id=M1 | name=Authentication (JWT) | path=taskflow/docs/specs/M1-auth.md
[SPEC] id=M2 | name=Task CRUD | path=taskflow/docs/specs/M2-task-crud.md
[SPEC] id=M3 | name=Task State Machine | path=taskflow/docs/specs/M3-task-state-machine.md
[SPEC] id=M6 | name=Task List Page (Vue) | path=taskflow/docs/specs/M6-task-list-page.md

[SECTION] Task Dependency Graph
[DEP] T1 → T2, T3 (parallel) → T4 → T5
[DEP] T6 → T7 (independent of backend chain)
[WAVE] 1=T1,T6 | 2=T2,T3,T7 | 3=T4 | 4=T5

content.content:
1→# Implementation Notes for T5: Task State Machine
     2→
     3→## Framework Pitfalls
     4→
     5→1. **No Lombok** - The project does NOT use Lombok (not in pom.xml). Write all getters/setters manually. Check `Task.java` for the pattern.
     6→
     7→2. **Exception handling pattern** - TaskController uses `@ExceptionHandler` at the controller level (not global). For the 400 "Cannot transition" error, you need to either:
     8→   - Add a new exception class (like `TaskService.TaskNotFoundException`) and handle it in TaskController
     9→   - Or throw an appropriate exception that gets mapped to 400
    10→   - The error format is `{"message": "..."}` — see `TaskController:75-78` for the pattern.
    11→
    12→3. **TaskService.toResponse()** is private — the `transition()` method should live in TaskStateMachine as a service that delegates to TaskRepository, OR the transition logic can be in TaskService using TaskStateMachine. Either way, the controller should return `TaskResponse` using the same `toResponse` mapping.
    13→
    14→4. **SecurityContextHolder.getContext().getAuthentication().getPrincipal()** returns `Long` (userId) — see `TaskController:41`. The new endpoints need authentication but don't need the userId for the state machine logic.
    15→
    16→5. **Soft delete** - Use `findByIdAndDeletedFalse(id)` for task lookup (see `TaskRepository`). Don't use `findById` or deleted tasks will be found.
    17→
    18→6. **updatedAt must be set** - On status transition, set `task.setUpdatedAt(LocalDateTime.now())` before saving. The `@PrePersist` only sets `createdAt`, there's no `@PreUpdate` — see `Task.java:56-59`.
    19→
    20→7. **PATCH endpoint path** - The contract says `PATCH /api/tasks/{id}/status`. Since TaskController is `@RequestMapping("/api/tasks")`, the method mapping should be `@PatchMapping("/{id}/status")`.
    21→
    22→8. **GET transitions path** - `GET /api/tasks/{id}/transitions` maps to `@GetMapping("/{id}/transitions")` on TaskController.
    23→
    24→9. **TransitionResponse.availableTransitions** is `List<String>` (not `List<TaskStatus>`). Convert enum values to strings: `status.name()`.
    25→
    26→10. **StatusChangeRequest.status** field uses `Task.TaskStatus` enum. Use `@jakarta.validation.constraints.NotNull` on it. Spring will handle enum deserialization from JSON string automatically.
    27→
    28→11. **TaskStateMachine should be a Spring @Service** so it can be injected. It does NOT need TaskRepository — it's pure transition logic. The `transition()` method that actually updates the task should be in TaskService (or TaskStateMachine with TaskRepository injected). The tests expect `TaskStateMachine` to be instantiable with `new TaskStateMachine()` for unit testing, so keep it simple with no constructor args.
    29→
    30→12. **Import for @PatchMapping** - Make sure to import `org.springframework.web.bind.annotation.PatchMapping` in TaskController.
    31→
    32→## Test Expectations
    33→
    34→- Unit test (`TaskStateMachineTest`): Calls `new TaskStateMachine()` directly — needs a no-arg constructor.
    35→  - Methods expected: `canTransition(TaskStatus from, TaskStatus to)` returns boolean, `getAvailableTransitions(TaskStatus status)` returns `List<TaskStatus>`.
    36→- Integration test (`StatusTransitionIntegrationTest`): Uses `PATCH /api/tasks/{id}/status` with body `{"status":"IN_PROGRESS"}` and `GET /api/tasks/{id}/transitions`.
    37→  - PATCH returns `TaskResponse` (same shape as POST/PUT responses).
    38→  - GET transitions returns `{"currentStatus":"TODO","availableTransitions":["IN_PROGRESS"]}`.
    39→  - Invalid transition returns 400 with `{"message":"Cannot transition from X to Y"}`.
    40→  - 404 for nonexistent task, 401 without token.
    41→

file.content:
# Implementation Notes for T5: Task State Machine

## Framework Pitfalls

1. **No Lombok** - The project does NOT use Lombok (not in pom.xml). Write all getters/setters manually. Check `Task.java` for the pattern.

2. **Exception handling pattern** - TaskController uses `@ExceptionHandler` at the controller level (not global). For the 400 "Cannot transition" error, you need to either:
   - Add a new exception class (like `TaskService.TaskNotFoundException`) and handle it in TaskController
   - Or throw an appropriate exception that gets mapped to 400
   - The error format is `{"message": "..."}` — see `TaskController:75-78` for the pattern.

3. **TaskService.toResponse()** is private — the `transition()` method should live in TaskStateMachine as a service that delegates to TaskRepository, OR the transition logic can be in TaskService using TaskStateMachine. Either way, the controller should return `TaskResponse` using the same `toResponse` mapping.

4. **SecurityContextHolder.getContext().getAuthentication().getPrincipal()** returns `Long` (userId) — see `TaskController:41`. The new endpoints need authentication but don't need the userId for the state machine logic.

5. **Soft delete** - Use `findByIdAndDeletedFalse(id)` for task lookup (see `TaskRepository`). Don't use `findById` or deleted tasks will be found.

6. **updatedAt must be set** - On status transition, set `task.setUpdatedAt(LocalDateTime.now())` before saving. The `@PrePersist` only sets `createdAt`, there's no `@PreUpdate` — see `Task.java:56-59`.

7. **PATCH endpoint path** - The contract says `PATCH /api/tasks/{id}/status`. Since TaskController is `@RequestMapping("/api/tasks")`, the method mapping should be `@PatchMapping("/{id}/status")`.

8. **GET transitions path** - `GET /api/tasks/{id}/transitions` maps to `@GetMapping("/{id}/transitions")` on TaskController.

9. **TransitionResponse.availableTransitions** is `List<String>` (not `List<TaskStatus>`). Convert enum values to strings: `status.name()`.

10. **StatusChangeRequest.status** field uses `Task.TaskStatus` enum. Use `@jakarta.validation.constraints.NotNull` on it. Spring will handle enum deserialization from JSON string automatically.

11. **TaskStateMachine should be a Spring @Service** so it can be injected. It does NOT need TaskRepository — it's pure transition logic. The `transition()` method that actually updates the task should be in TaskService (or TaskStateMachine with TaskRepository injected). The tests expect `TaskStateMachine` to be instantiable with `new TaskStateMachine()` for unit testing, so keep it simple with no constructor args.

12. **Import for @PatchMapping** - Make sure to import `org.springframework.web.bind.annotation.PatchMapping` in TaskController.

## Test Expectations

- Unit test (`TaskStateMachineTest`): Calls `new TaskStateMachine()` directly — needs a no-arg constructor.
  - Methods expected: `canTransition(TaskStatus from, TaskStatus to)` returns boolean, `getAvailableTransitions(TaskStatus status)` returns `List<TaskStatus>`.
- Integration test (`StatusTransitionIntegrationTest`): Uses `PATCH /api/tasks/{id}/status` with body `{"status":"IN_PROGRESS"}` and `GET /api/tasks/{id}/transitions`.
  - PATCH returns `TaskResponse` (same shape as POST/PUT responses).
  - GET transitions returns `{"currentStatus":"TODO","availableTransitions":["IN_PROGRESS"]}`.
  - Invalid transition returns 400 with `{"message":"Cannot transition from X to Y"}`.
  - 404 for nonexistent task, 401 without token.

input.content:
# Worker-5 Log: T5 - M3 Task State Machine

## 16:05 - Started
- Read PLAN, CONTRACT, impl-notes-5, test files, and all READONLY source files
- Understood existing patterns: TaskController structure, TaskService.toResponse(), TaskNotFoundException, findByIdAndDeletedFalse

## 16:06 - Implementation
Created 3 new files + modified 1:

1. **TaskStateMachine.java** - @Component with static EnumMap for transitions. No-arg constructor (unit tests use `new`). Methods: `canTransition(from, to)`, `getAvailableTransitions(status)`.
2. **StatusChangeRequest.java** - DTO with `@NotNull TaskStatus status` field + getter/setter.
3. **TransitionResponse.java** - DTO with `String currentStatus` + `List<String> availableTransitions` + getters/setters.
4. **TaskController.java** (modified) - Added `TaskStateMachine` + `TaskRepository` injection. Added `PATCH /{id}/status` and `GET /{id}/transitions` endpoints. Used inline toResponse conversion (TaskService.toResponse is private).

## 16:07 - Tests
- Ran `mvn test -Dtest="TaskStateMachineTest,StatusTransitionIntegrationTest"`
- Result: **41 tests run, 0 failures, 0 errors, 0 skipped** — BUILD SUCCESS
- Verified changed files against ALLOWED list — all in scope

## Status: DONE
